commands.repair_reason: The protected maintainer label, public API semantics, proof gate, and prompt trust-boundary decision need human review rather than an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff is supply-chain clean, but it changes a prompt trust boundary and needs maintainer review before merge. commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves configured group system prompts, adds an optional `untrustedGroupSystemPrompt` supplemental fact that is converted into untrusted structured context, and extends focused inbound-context tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level boundary, but not as a confirmed bug: current main sanitizes user-controlled body fields while leaving configured `GroupSystemPrompt` on the trusted prompt path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label, public API semantics, proof gate, and prompt trust-boundary decision need human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: sanitize group prompt inbound text [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Group prompt text was not normalized through the same inbound text sanitizer as other message-controlled prompt fields.
- Solution: Route `GroupSystemPrompt` through the existing inbound text normalization and marker neutralization helper.
- What changed: Added sanitizer coverage in `finalizeInboundContext` and extended the inbound context regression test.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No prompt assembly, channel routing, plugin config, or command behavior changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- N/A
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Motivation

- Group prompt context should follow the same inbound text normalization rules as other message-controlled text fields before prompt assembly.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Group prompt text with line-leading role-like markers is neutralized during inbound context finalization.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout test environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Test Files  1 passed (1)` and `Tests  56 passed (56)`.
- Observed result after fix: The targeted inbound test suite passed, including coverage for `GroupSystemPrompt` sanitization.
- What was not tested: Live channel runtime behavior and full repository checks.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `finalizeInboundContext` normalized several inbound text fields but did not apply the same helper to `GroupSystemPrompt`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing sanitizer regression coverage did not include the group prompt field.
- Contributing context (if known): Group prompt text is later included in assembled prompt context, so it needs the same inbound text handling as adjacent fields.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `GroupSystemPrompt` content with a line-leading marker is normalized to the neutralized form.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior belongs to the shared inbound context finalizer, so a focused unit test covers all callers using that boundary.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

```text
Before:
[channel/plugin group prompt] -> [inbound context finalization] -> [prompt assembly]

After:
[channel/plugin group prompt] -> [inbound text sanitizer] -> [inbound context finalization] -> [prompt assembly]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Shared inbound context path
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Add a `GroupSystemPrompt` value containing a line-leading role-like marker to an inbound context fixture.
2. Finalize the context through `finalizeInboundContext`.
3. Confirm the finalized group prompt contains the neutralized marker text.

### Expected

- `GroupSystemPrompt` is normalized with the existing inbound text sanitizer.

### Actual

- `GroupSystemPrompt` is normalized with the existing inbound text sanitizer.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Targeted inbound context unit test for group prompt sanitization.
- Edge cases checked: Multiline prompt text with a line-leading marker.
- What you did **not** verify: Live channel runtime behavior, full suite, broad changed checks.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

None.

AI-assisted: Yes
